data.title: Wall St expects Argentina market boost from IMF deal, big shift on FX controls
data.description:
Wall Street investors are forecasting a positive market reaction in Argentina after the country sealed a $20 billion loan program with the International Monetary Fund and undid large parts of its currency and capital controls late on Friday.

Traders expect bonds and equities to rise on Monday, with Argentina's risk index that reflects investor sentiment in the country's debt falling. The IMF support is seen boosting overall confidence and the removal of controls helping spur investment.

The peso is likely to weaken after the central bank undid its so-called "crawling peg" and shifted to a far wider trading band of 1,000-1,400 pesos per dollar, though this could be offset by increased demand for pesos from grain exporters.

The currency had closed at 1,074 per dollar, though popular parallel rates (ARSB=) often used by Argentines and local firms to access greenbacks due to strict capital controls in place since 2019 had been nearer 1,350 per dollar.

"We would expect a positive market reaction to the announcements made on Friday," investment bank Goldman Sachs said in a note on Sunday, adding that the new floating exchange rate "exceeded our expectations".

"(This) can positively contribute to the medium-term sustainability of the macroeconomic adjustment program being implemented in Argentina," it added.

The IMF deal will release an initial $12 billion, with $3 billion more coming later in the year. Argentina also announced large loan deals with other multinational lenders and banks that should help bolster its depleted foreign currency reserves.

The South American grains producer is digging itself out of a major economic crisis under libertarian President Javier Milei, who came to office in late 2023 and has managed to stabilize the economy with austerity and fiscal discipline.

The IMF deal came with targets and baseline assumptions that would see the country continue to commit to a "zero deficit" and to build up reserves this year. New investment in areas like energy and grains exports will be key for that.

J.P. Morgan said that the likely fall in the peso could be tempered by demand from grains exporters for pesos as they looked to liquidate their foreign currency income at a more attractive exchange rate.

"In our view, the official FX will likely stabilize below the parallel FX level as of Friday, with agriculture related FX supply catching up. The FX gap will likely shrink to around 5%," the investment bank said.

"The policy advancements represent a significant step forward, enabling the country to unlock a potential that has been stifled for decades due to poor policy-making."

Dear Sirs

Sydbank share buyback programme: transactions in week 15

On 26 February 2025 Sydbank announced a share buyback programme of DKK 1,350m. The share buyback programme commenced on 3 March 2025 and will be completed by 31 January 2026.

The purpose of the share buyback programme is to reduce the share capital of Sydbank and the programme is executed in compliance with the provisions of Regulation (EU) No 596/2014 of the European Parliament and of the Council of 16 April 2014 and Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052 of 8 March 2016, collectively referred to as the Safe Harbour rules.

data.title: Correction: Vår Energi ASA publishes the Annual Report for 2024
data.description:
Vår Energi ASA

Sandnes, Norway, 14 April 2025: Reference is made to the release issued 31 March 2025 related to Vår Energi Annual Report for 2024.

The updated report contains a small change in the wording in the responsibility statement on page 249.

There are no changes to the numbers reported in the financial statements of 2024.

+47 92616759

andreas.wulff@varenergi.no

About Vår Energi

 

Vår Energi is a leading independent upstream oil and gas company on the Norwegian continental shelf (NCS). We are committed to deliver a better future through responsible value driven growth based on over 50 years of NCS operations, a robust and diversified asset portfolio with ongoing development projects, and a strong exploration track record.

Safe and responsible operations are at the core of our strategy. Our ambition is to be the safest operator on the NCS, and to become carbon neutral in our net equity operational emissions by 2030.

Vår Energi has around 1400 employees and equity stakes in 42 producing fields. We have our headquarters outside Stavanger, Norway, with offices in Oslo, Hammerfest and Florø. To learn more, please visit varenergi.no.

data.title: PTA-CMS: Frequentis AG: Envisaged disposal of own share within the scope of the Long Term Incentive Plan 2022
data.description:
Frequentis AG

by Stefan Marin

Announcement according to article 119 section 9 BörseG Vienna, Austria (pta/14.04.2025/10:53) - The Executive Board of Frequentis AG has resolved today, subject to the approval of the Supervisory Board, to make use of its authorization granted by resolution of the Ordinary General Meeting of Frequentis AG from 6 June 2024 to dispose of, or utilize, own shares, inter alia, to grant own shares to members of the Executive Board of the Company, in order to be able to allocate own shares in the Company to the Chairman of the Executive Board of the Company in accordance with the Long Term Incentive Plan 2022 ("LTIP2022") approved by resolution of the Ordinary General Meeting of Frequentis AG from 2 June 2022 and to exclude the general purchasing possibility of the shareholders of the Company in connection therewith. Accordingly, subject to the approval of the Supervisory Board, a maximum number of 18,000 of own shares in the Company (gross number before deduction of taxes and duties) shall be transferred to the Chairman of the Executive Board of the Company within the scope of the LTIP2022. In this respect, the general purchasing possibility of the shareholders shall be excluded with respect to the own shares required for the LTIP2022 in accordance with statutory requirements and subject to the approval of the Supervisory Board. The actual number of own shares which shall be transferred to the Chairman of the Executive Board of the Company within the scope of the LTIP2022 depends on the actual target achievement by the Chairman of the Executive Board of the Company under the LTIP2022 and is expected to be determined by the Executive Board at the beginning of May 2025 after the determination of the target achievement by the Supervisory Board and its approval of the execution of the disposal of own shares as well as of the report of the Executive Board and the Supervisory Board regarding the exclusion of the general purchasing possibility of the shareholders published today on the website of the Company registered in the commercial register. About FREQUENTIS Frequentis stands for a safer world. Our solutions are used in our customers' command and control centres to help them make the world safer. Frequentis is a world leader in high-tech solutions for Air Traffic Management, supporting both civil and military air traffic control organisations, as well as Public Safety and Transport, where police, emergency rescue services, fire brigades, railways, coastguards, and port authorities rely on our extensive portfolio. The listed family business based in Vienna, Austria, drives innovative and sustainable solutions for safety and security in everyday life and in the safety-critical sector. Its air traffic optimisation solutions for air traffic control centres are contributing to reducing emissions. As a global player with more than 2,400 employees (full-time equivalents/FTE), Frequentis has a worldwide network of companies in over 50 countries. Its products, services, and solutions are used in around 150 countries. Shares in Frequentis are traded on the Vienna and Frankfurt stock exchanges; ISIN: ATFREQUENT09, WKN: A2PHG5. In 2024, revenues were EUR 480.3 million and EBIT was EUR 32.1 million. For more information, please visit http://www.frequentis.com Barbara Fürchtegott, Head of Communications / Company Spokesperson barbara.fuerchtegott@frequentis.com, +43 1 81150-4631 Stefan Marin, Head of Investor Relations stefan.marin@frequentis.com, +43 1 81150-1074 (end) emitter: Frequentis AG address: Innovationsstraße 1, 1100 Wien country: Austria contact person: Stefan Marin phone: +431811501074 e-mail: stefan.marin@frequentis.com website: www.frequentis.com ISIN(s): ATFREQUENT09 (share) stock exchanges: official trade in Vienna other stock exchanges: Regulierter Markt in Frankfurt pressetext Nachrichtenagentur GmbH Ad-hoc and financial news distributed by pressetext.adhoc. data.title: Envisaged disposal of own share within the scope of the Long Term Incentive Plan 2022
data.description:
Frequentis AG

by Stefan Marin

Announcement according to article 119 section 9 BörseG

Frequentis AG: Envisaged disposal of own share within the scope of the Long Term Incentive Plan 2022

Vienna, Austria (pta000/14.04.2025/10:53 UTC+2)

The Executive Board of Frequentis AG has resolved today, subject to the approval of the Supervisory Board, to make use of its authorization granted by resolution of the Ordinary General Meeting of Frequentis AG from 6 June 2024 to dispose of, or utilize, own shares, inter alia, to grant own shares to members of the Executive Board of the Company, in order to be able to allocate own shares in the Company to the Chairman of the Executive Board of the Company in accordance with the Long Term Incentive Plan 2022 ("LTIP2022") approved by resolution of the Ordinary General Meeting of Frequentis AG from 2 June 2022 and to exclude the general purchasing possibility of the shareholders of the Company in connection therewith.

Accordingly, subject to the approval of the Supervisory Board, a maximum number of 18,000 of own shares in the Company (gross number before deduction of taxes and duties) shall be transferred to the Chairman of the Executive Board of the Company within the scope of the LTIP2022. In this respect, the general purchasing possibility of the shareholders shall be excluded with respect to the own shares required for the LTIP2022 in accordance with statutory requirements and subject to the approval of the Supervisory Board.

The actual number of own shares which shall be transferred to the Chairman of the Executive Board of the Company within the scope of the LTIP2022 depends on the actual target achievement by the Chairman of the Executive Board of the Company under the LTIP2022 and is expected to be determined by the Executive Board at the beginning of May 2025 after the determination of the target achievement by the Supervisory Board and its approval of the execution of the disposal of own shares as well as of the report of the Executive Board and the Supervisory Board regarding the exclusion of the general purchasing possibility of the shareholders published today on the website of the Company registered in the commercial register.

About FREQUENTIS

Frequentis stands for a safer world. Our solutions are used in our customers' command and control centres to help them make the world safer.

Frequentis is a world leader in high-tech solutions for Air Traffic Management, supporting both civil and military air traffic control organisations, as well as Public Safety and Transport, where police, emergency rescue services, fire brigades, railways, coastguards, and port authorities rely on our extensive portfolio.

The listed family business based in Vienna, Austria, drives innovative and sustainable solutions for safety and security in everyday life and in the safety-critical sector. Its air traffic optimisation solutions for air traffic control centres are contributing to reducing emissions.

As a global player with more than 2,400 employees (full-time equivalents/FTE), Frequentis has a worldwide network of companies in over 50 countries. Its products, services, and solutions are used in around 150 countries. Shares in Frequentis are traded on the Vienna and Frankfurt stock exchanges; ISIN: ATFREQUENT09, WKN: A2PHG5. In 2024, revenues were EUR 480.3 million and EBIT was EUR 32.1 million.

For more information, please visit www.frequentis.com

data.title: Montenegro extends deadline in new tender for Bar-Boljare motorway section
data.description:
PODGORICA (Montenegro), April 14 (SeeNews) - Montenegro has extended to May 14 from April 29 the bidding deadline in the new tender for the design and construction of the 22-km Matesevo-Andrijevica section of the planned Bar-Boljare motorway, the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD), which is co-financing the project, said.

No reason for the extension was provided in the tender invitation published on the EBRD's website.

The tender was relaunched in late February after the EBRD and the European Union, acting as its main financiers, requested the cancellation of the first procedure due to route revisions prompted by local protests and because the previous shortlist included only firms from China, Turkey, and Azerbaijan, local media reported at the time.

The construction of the Matesevo-Andrijevica section, valued at 605 million euro ($686 million), is a major project for Montenegro. The tender procedures have been supervised by the EBRD, which is providing a 200 million euro loan to support the project. In addition, the European Commission has committed 100 million euro in grant funding, with an option to approve a second tranche of 100 million euro, while Montenegro plans to provide 153 million euro from its own funds.

The initial two-stage pre-qualification tender was opened in April 2024, attracting interest from nine companies - Austria's Strabag AG, Power Construction Corporation of China (PCCC), Turkish firms Dogus and Cengiz, Azerbaijan's Azvirt, China's Shandong Hi-Speed Group (SDHS), North Macedonia's Gradjevinski Institut Makedonija (GIM), China Communications Construction Company (CCCC), and Baybnet Group. In October, transport minister Maja Vukicevic said Montenegro had shortlisted four of them - three Chinese firms and a Turkish-Azerbaijani company.

Matesevo-Andrijevica will be the second section of the Bar-Boljare motorway that will link the Adriatic port of Bar in Montenegro's south with the country's northern border with Serbia. The first section - i.e. the 41-km Smokovac-Matesevo stretch, which was built by China Road and Bridge Corporation (CRBC), was opened for traffic in July 2022.

In February, Montenegro hired a local consortium comprising CDS Project, Via Project and Geotin MNE to prepare the preliminary design of the 50-km Andrijevica-Berane-Boljare section, which is the third planned stretch of the Bar-Boljare motorway. A fourth section is to link Djurmani at the Adriatic town of Bar to Smokovac near the capital Podgorica.

The Bar-Boljare motorway is part of European transport Corridor XI, a ferry/motorway route connecting Italy's Bari to Romania's Bucharest via Montenegro's Bar and Serbia's Belgrade.

($ = 0.881 euro)

data.title: Spot prices down on soaring wind supply
data.description:
Strong gains in wind power supply throughout the region put bearish pressure on spot prices on Monday, as the surge offset growing demand and lower solar power supply.

German baseload power for Tuesday (TRDEBD1) was at 67.50 euros ($76.83) per megawatt hour (MWh) by 0845 GMT, while the day-ahead French power (TRFRBD1) was at 18.25 euros/MWh, LSEG data showed.

These were down from closing prices of 103.75 euros and 68 euros respectively last Monday. Neither contract traded on Friday for Monday delivery.

"Wind surges again (on Tuesday), painting a firmly bearish outlook for German power prices," said LSEG analyst Riccardo Parviero.

Wind generation is expected to stay strong in the first part of the day then taper off in the evening. The strong growth is seen completely offsetting the increase in demand and drop in solar supplies, he added.

German wind power output was expected to jump by 16.7 gigawatts (GW) to 22.1 GW on Tuesday while French output was projected to rise by 5.9 GW to 8 GW, LSEG data showed.

However, German solar generation was expected to fall by 1.4 GW on Tuesday to 12.1 GW, LSEG data showed.

Power consumption in Germany is projected to increase by 1.9 GW to 55.1 GW on Tuesday, while demand in France is forecast to rise by 1.4 GW to 43.4 GW.

French nuclear availability rose three percentage point to 74% of total capacity as three reactors returned from outages.

German year-ahead baseload power (TRDEBYZ6) rose 0.6% to 81.20 euros/MWh, while the French equivalent (TRFRBYZ6) had a bid ask range between 61.25 euros to 62.15 euros.

In the European carbon market (CFI2Zc1), the benchmark contract was up 1.7% at 65.89 euros a metric ton.

($1 = 0.8786 euros)

data.title: Soybeans Hold at 7-Week Highs
data.description:
Soybean futures held steady around $10.40 per bushel, their highest level since February 20, after gaining 6.7% for the week.

The rally was driven by President Trump's decision to pause some planned tariff hikes and a revised U.S. soybean supply outlook from the USDA. A sharply weaker dollar, pressured by intensifying U.S.-China trade tensions, added further support.

On Friday, China’s commerce ministry announced a tariff increase on all U.S. imports to 125%, in response to President Trump’s move a day earlier to raise tariffs on Chinese goods to 145%.

Meanwhile, tariffs on other major trading partners were scaled back to a base rate of 10%.

The USDA lowered its 2024–25 U.S. soybean ending stocks estimate to 375 million bushels, down from 380 million and below market expectations, while increasing its forecast for global soybean inventories.

data.title: Stocks rally as electronics get a tariff break
data.description:
Key points:
  • Wall St futures up as US tariffs delayed on some electronics
  • Confusion as White House says levies might still come
  • Tech stocks outperform in Europe and Asia
  • Dollar, Treasuries vulnerable to loss of confidence

Major stock indexes bounced in Europe and Asia on Monday after the White House exempted smartphones and computers from "reciprocal" U.S. tariffs, though gains were limited as President Donald Trump warned levies were still likely.

Indeed, Trump on Sunday told reporters tariffs on semiconductors would be announced over the next week and a decision on phones made "soon".

On the face of it, the exemption of 20 product types accounting for 23% of U.S. imports from China was a boon to manufacturers.

However, the uncertainty was still exerting downward pressure on the dollar and U.S. government bonds, and the off-again, on-again trade policy gyrations have left investors confused and analysts bearish on the long run.

"I think the likelihood that deals get agreed and lead to lower tariffs down the road has grown a bit, and I feel relatively good about the news flow," said Samy Chaar, chief economist at Lombard Odier.

"Still the risk scenario of things breaking down hasn't gone away, we're still worried about both the overall level of tariffs and also how long we remain with this uncertainty because that itself is causing damage."

"If you're a U.S. company, you've postponed hiring, and capital expenditure, and I worry about when companies have to start cutting costs, that's the point you don't want to reach."

Still, just the prospect of a pause was enough to lift S&P 500 futures 1.4%, while Nasdaq futures rose 1.8%. The S&P 500 rallied 5.7% last week, but was still more than 5% below where it was before the reciprocal tariffs were first announced in early April.

The optimism was also felt in Europe and Asia, outperforming also because they missed the tail end of the bounce on Wall Street on Friday.

Europe's broad STOXX 600 index rose 2%, having lost 2% last week, and MSCI's broadest index of Asia-Pacific shares outside Japan (.MIAPJ0000PUS) gained 1.5% after shedding more than 4% last week.

Tech firms and the broader supply chain were the biggest gainers, up 2.8% in Europe, after giants in Apple's supply chain surged in Asia.

Apple's own shares jumped nearly 6% in very early premarket trading.

The market also has more earnings to weather this week with Goldman Sachs, Bank of America and Citigroup among the big banks reporting. Numbers from chipmaker TSMC will be a highlight given Trump's plan to investigate the entire global semiconductor supply chain.

In terms of economic data, March numbers showed a 12.4% jump in Chinese exports during March as firms rushed in orders ahead of Trump's tariffs.

On the docket this week are U.S. retail sales and Chinese gross domestic product, while Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell speaks on the economic outlook on Wednesday, when he will almost certainly be quizzed on the prospect of rate cuts and the recent stress in the Treasury market.

NOT SO SAFE

Early on Monday, there was scant sign of any recovery in U.S. bonds with 10-year yields at 4.47% , having seen the largest weekly rise in borrowing costs in decades.

Notably the rise in yields came alongside a fall in the dollar both on safe havens like the yen and Swiss franc, but also the euro.

The fall can be explained overseas investors flooding out of U.S. assets to move back home, though some are asking broader questions.

"It is no longer hyperbole to say that the dollar's reserve status and broader dominant role is at least somewhat in question, even if the inertia and network effects that have kept the dollar on top for decades are not going away any time soon," said Jonas Goltermann, deputy chief markets economist at Capital Economics.

"The key questions are around the indirect damage done through generating extreme uncertainty around the policy and economic outlook, the ongoing dislocations in the Treasury market and, ultimately, undermining confidence in U.S. institutions and asset markets."

Japanese officials are gearing up for trade negotiations with the United States that will likely touch on currency policy, with some officials privately bracing for Washington to call on Tokyo to prop up the yen.

They might not need to work too hard given the dollar had taken a beating from worries the erratic nature of Trump's trade policy was shaking investor faith in U.S. assets.

On Monday, the dollar was last down another 0.4% on the Japanese currency, below 143 yen after hitting a six-month low at 142.05 last week. It was pinned at 0.8174 Swiss francs , having shed more than 5% last week to the lowest in a decade.

The euro was a touch firmer $1.1354 , just short of a three-year top of $1.1474. The European Central Bank meets on Thursday and is considered certain to cut rates by a quarter point to 2.25%. (0#EURIRPR)

Canada's central bank also meets this week, and markets imply around a one-in-three chance it might trim its 2.75% rates. (0#CADIRPR)

In commodity markets, global uncertainty was proving a windfall to gold prices which surged to all-time peaks at $3,245 an ounce on Monday.

Oil has had a much tougher time amid fears of a global economic slowdown and increased supply from OPEC, though it found some support from the risk of an end to Iran's exports.

Brent was up 23 cents at $64.99 a barrel, while U.S. crude nudged up 8 cents to $61.58 per barrel.

data.title: BP Discovers Oil at U.S. Far South Prospect in Gulf of Mexico
data.description:
By Adam Whittaker

BP said it has discovered oil at the U.S. Far South prospect in the Gulf of Mexico at a time when the British energy major is growing upstream production under a reset strategy.

BP said Monday initial data shows high-quality reservoirs and a potentially commercial volume of hydrocarbons.

The exploration well is approximately 120 miles off the coast of Louisiana and was drilled to total depth of 23,830 feet, BP said.

The discovery demonstrates BP's increased investment in exploration and strengthens its upstream portfolio, the company said.

Under its reset strategy and renewed focus on hydrocarbon production, BP expects to grow its global upstream production to 2.3 million to 2.5 million barrels of oil equivalent per day in 2030, with the option to increase production through 2035.

BP is planning on drilling around 40 wells over the next three years, with 10 to 15 of those to be drilled in 2025, the company said.

BP co-owns the Far South prospect with Chevron.

Write to Adam Whittaker at adam.whittaker@wsj.com

data.title: Paraxylene (PX) prices plummet in Europe
data.description: Last week, PX prices nosedived in the European region.An industry source in Europe, wishing to remain unidentified, informed a Polymerupdate team member, "This week, the European paraxylene (PX) spot market faced increased uncertainty following tariff announcements from the Trump administration. The market experienced significant volatility as participants reacted to these policy changes and speculated on their effects on global trade dynamics. Spot prices showed some correlation with fluctuations in the Asian PX market, which heightened concerns among market players. A key issue for European stakeholders is the limited number of operational purified terephthalic acid (PTA) producers in the region. With domestic demand for PX already restricted, the additional strain from U.S. tariff policies has made it increasingly challenging for European PX manufacturers to sell their products. Although the U.S. later declared a temporary 90-day delay on the new tariffs, the European market remained cautious about a possible shift of Asian paraxylene shipments towards Europe. Market sources opined that with tariffs imposed on PX imports into the U.S., it is inevitable that more Asian material will be redirected to Europe. However, Europe is already oversupplied with PX. The source said, "Interestingly, reports indicated that PX originating from Europe was being shipped to the U.S. this week, hinting at temporary arbitrage opportunities amid the evolving global trade scenario. In contrast, the European orthoxylene (OX) spot market maintained relative stability during the same timeframe. Although demand remains subdued�primarily due to balanced-to-tight market conditions�price fluctuations were minimal. Market participants noted that OX displayed significantly less volatility compared to mixed xylenes (MX) or paraxylene. While the spot market is not particularly active, it has shown greater stability this week compared to MX or PX, one participant observed, indicating that orthoxylene appears to be somewhat shielded from the wider market disruptions for the time being. European PX manufacturers are expected to encounter increasing challenges in the future as prolonged supply conditions continue and global trade dynamics adjust due to tariff regulations. A rise in Asian exports to Europe may further exert downward pressure on regional prices unless there is a recovery in demand from PTA or other downstream industries. At the same time, the orthoxylene market is anticipated to remain stable in the short term, unless significant shifts occur in regional supply-demand dynamics or unforeseen economic disruptions arise.On Friday, FOB Rotterdam PX prices were assessed at the USD 740-750/mt levels, a tumble of USD (-85/mt) from week on week.Meanwhile in Asia, PX prices on Friday were assessed at the USD 710-720/mt FOB Korea levels, a steep week on week drop of USD (-70/mt).

data.title: Release of capital market information
data.description:
Nexus AG

by Hannes Wehinger

Announcement according to article 2 of the Regulation (EC) 2016/1052

Nexus AG: Release of capital market information

Interim Reporting 90

Donaueschingen (pta000/14.04.2025/10:51 UTC+2)

In the time period from April 7, 2025 until and including April 11, 2025, no shares were bought back within the framework of the share buy-back program of NEXUS AG, whose start was disclosed by NEXUS AG on July 24, 2023.

The transactions in a detailed form are published on the website of NEXUS AG (www.nexus-ag.de - Investor Relations – Stock Buyback).

The total volume of shares which have been bought back within the framework of the share buy-back (share buyback program 2023) amounts to 63,432 shares.

The purchase of the shares of NEXUS AG is carried out by a credit institution that has been commissioned by NEXUS AG; the shares are repurchased exclusively on the electronic trading platform of the Frankfurt Stock Exchange (Xetra).

Donaueschingen, 14.04.2025

NEXUS AG

The Managing Board

data.title: Jubilee Metals Appoints Successor to Retiring Chairperson
data.description:
Jubilee Metals Group's (, JBL.JO) Chairperson Ollie Oliveira plans to step down, effective April 30, according to a Monday filing.

Mathews Phosa, who currently serves as the vice-chairperson of the metals processing company's board, will succeed Oliveira, effective immediately.

The company also appointed interim Finance Director Jonathan Morley-Kirk to the permanent role.

data.title: Thailand Seeks U.S. Tariff Relief Ahead of April 21 Talks
data.description:
Thailand’s finance and commerce ministers will lead a delegation to the U.S. to seek relief from a planned 36% tariff under President Trump’s trade measures, a government spokesperson said on Monday.

An advance team, led by Finance Minister Pichai Chunhavajira, will depart Thursday for meetings with private sector groups, with Commerce Minister Pichai Naripthaphan joining later.

Talks with U.S. officials are expected on April 21.

Thailand, which exported USD 55 billion to the U.S. last year, faces pressure over a USD 45.6 billion trade deficit. "The strategy will focus on industries of mutual interest, such as pet food, opening markets, and increasing U.S. imports," according to spokesperson Jirayu Houngsub.

He also noted tighter inspections to prevent third-country circumvention.

Thailand has previously pledged to increase U.S. imports, lower corn tariffs, and boost joint investments in the U.S.

data.title: Fujio Food Group Discloses Parent Company FM Commercial Planning's Financial Results
data.description:
Fujio Food Group reported the financial performance of its unlisted parent company, FM Commercial Planning, which included a net loss of 39 billion yen for the year ended Dec. 31, 2024.

FM Commercial Planning posted net sales of 70.16 million yen, operating losses of 25.9 million yen, and ordinary losses of 38.8 million yen.

The Osaka-based parent company, led by Director Masahiro Fujio, who is also President of Fujio Food Group, has 3 million yen in share capital and administrative expenses of 90.89 million yen, offsetting a gross profit of 65 million yen.

FM Commercial Planning's non-operating expenses of 14.2 million yen further contributed to losses, despite a minor non-operating income of 1.25 million yen.

FM Commercial Planning, which manages fitness clubs and holds 25.83% of Fujio Food's voting rights, including 12.54% indirectly, according to its bourse filing last Friday.
